title: buddleja tibetica var. farreri
intro: Buddleja tibetica var. farreri, also known as the Tibetan or Farrer's Buddleja, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. In traditional herbal medicine, Buddleja tibetica var. farreri is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts have also been incorporated into various pharmaceutical products and health supplements.
